Motion To Dismiss Punitive Damages Claim From Complaint Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 581(F)(4); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Cherie J. Edson
Off Calendar Jan-19-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT NITE CAP BAR, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss Punitive Damages Claim From Complaint Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 581(F)(4).

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' unopposed motion to dismiss punitive damages claim from complaint is granted. On October 27, 2021, this court granted Defendants' motion to strike the claim for punitive damages with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs did not amend the complaint within the time allowed by the Court. Accordingly, dismissal of Plaintiffs' punitive damages claim is warranted. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 581(f)(4); see Leader v. Health Industries of America, Inc. (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 603, 613-614.)

Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-18-2021 Continued To Feb-08-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Oct-27-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. SHAWN SASOONESS, Esq. and AMIR K. NEVIS, Esq. of Sasooness Law Group, APC (Tel: 818-922-7700) for plaintiff Albert Malbrough;
Atty. CHERIE J. EDSON, Esq. of Severson & Werson (Tel: 415-398-3344) for defendant Joseph Summa.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JOSEPH SUMMA'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Specially appearing Defendant Joseph Summa's motion to quash is granted. "On a challenge to personal jurisdiction by a motion to quash, the plaintiff has the burden of proving, by a preponderance of the evidence, the factual bases justifying the exercise of jurisdiction." (ViaView, Inc. v. Retzlaff (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th 198, 209-210.) Pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. sec. 415.20(b), a plaintiff may substitute serve an individual at his "usual place of business." In this case, the weight of the evidence demonstrates that defendant Summa no longer had a business relationship with the Nite Cap Bar, Inc. at the time of service. (Summa Decl., pars. 3-4, 6-7.) Substitute service at the bar therefore was not proper and plaintiff fails to meet his burden. Plaintiff's waiver argument makes no sense since a motion to quash pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. sec. 418.10 is the proper vehicle to contest a lack of jurisdiction. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 418.10(d) [motion to quash is not a general appearance].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-27-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. SHAWN SASOONESS, Esq. and AMIR K. NEVIS, Esq. of Sasooness Law Group, APC (Tel: 818-922-7700) for plaintiff Albert Malbrough;
Atty. CHERIE J. EDSON, Esq. of Severson & Werson (Tel: 415-398-3344) for defendant Joseph Summa.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS NITE CAP BAR, INC., DAVID ESLER, AND DAVID LINSMAYER'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' (Nite Cap Bar, Inc., Esler, and Linsmayer) motion to strike the claim for punitive damages is granted with 20 days leave to amend. A party seeking punitive damages must allege specific facts to support the prayer. (See G.D. Searle & Co. v. Superior Court (1975) 49 Cal.App.3d 22, 29; Cyrus v. Haveson (1976) 65 Cal.App.3d 306, 316-317.) Here, plaintiff's allegations are conclusory and do not plead sufficient ultimate facts regarding how defendants authorized or ratified the attack or were on notice that such an attack would ensue but consciously disregarded the danger. (See Complaint, pars. 30, 35, 40.) In addition, the claim for punitive damages on the premises liability claim is uncertain. The complaint does not highlight any physical defect in the property. Plaintiff apparently avers that the employees had a known propensity for violence which rendered the property dangerous. Plaintiff has leave to plead ultimate facts showing how the corporate employer and individuals acted maliciously within the meaning of Civil Code sec. 3294.

Order signed.
